comment.body:
while all these sounds fancy and clever even 'superior'
in itself it doesn't mean anything. 

the reason why it is so popular is that most furus want to sound like university professeurrs, talking some alien language,
and most retail newbie thinks he found the holy grail.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
its history goes back to 2021 gamma squeeze, when gamstop squeezed hard,
and that was the time when the retail crowd heard the first time about gamma squeeze.

all of a sudden, tons of gamma data providers came in with naive models,
then came Cem Karsan with his mysterious vanna/charm flows and OpEx cycle mantras,

and a cult started to emerge around market makers, seeing them as if they were some mysterious sacred priests of the market who know everything and move the trends.

then every second furu suddenly became ex-market maker, and it was all a good marketing to make retails believe that they finally can find their free lunch, or discount lunch

comment.body:
first of all, 
even tho these flows take out like 80% (according to some new researches even 90%) of the daily flows... these flows are not solely dealer hedging flows, but automatizes portfolio rebalancings, vol following strats, beta trades etc.
comment.raw_body:
first of all, 
even tho these flows take out like 80% (according to some new researches even 90%) of the daily flows... these flows are not solely dealer hedging flows, but automatizes portfolio rebalancings, vol following strats, beta trades etc.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
second,
market maker hedging flow is double negative in terms of direction.
if you bet on upside, and buy a call, your dealer will be short delta on his sold call position, so he will buy.

so the net customer positioning gets leverage through dealer flows.
comment.raw_body:
second,
market maker hedging flow is double negative in terms of direction.
if you bet on upside, and buy a call, your dealer will be short delta on his sold call position, so he will buy.

so the net customer positioning gets leverage through dealer flows.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
as you already know, 
trading is a game of probabilities. if you like it or not, it is and will be.
the sooner you get familiar with the concept of probability, the sooner you will be more conscious trader.
I dropped educational posts about that
comment.raw_body:
as you already know, 
trading is a game of probabilities. if you like it or not, it is and will be.
the sooner you get familiar with the concept of probability, the sooner you will be more conscious trader.
I dropped educational posts about that

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
but for now, to keep it simple...
probability is the ratio between the possibilities favoring to you and the all possible outcomes.

like lets say you do a monte carlo, that gives you the possible paths for the day.
lets say, you got 100 possible paths,
but for your current strategy, only 22 of that would be profitable.

so the probability is 22/100 = 0.22; multiply by 100 and you get the %
comment.raw_body:
